compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Design provenance (2026-06-30): finalized via the joe-brainstorm protocol — OSS prior-art research (ESLint/Semgrep/RuboCop/Danger/OpenRewrite custom-rule registries; ArchUnit/dependency-cruiser/import-linter/deptrac invariant tools; Semgrep Structure-Mode / SonarQube / LangSmith Align-Evals authoring+calibration UX) + two codebase-grounding validation passes. Key grounded findings: (1) .rebar/detectors/ and .rebar/evals/ ALREADY give project-rule and project-fixture discovery, and the editor already authors criterion prompts — so net-new work is concentrated in the gate CONSUMER/wiring/preview seams, not authoring/engine. (2) criteria_routing.json is hand-maintained (its 'derived' docstring is aspirational; no generator/drift-gate). (3) claim_gate_check never re-compares the routing version, so attestation invalidation on criteria change must be added. (4) RISK: code-review (b744) is mid-rewrite adding a PARALLEL registry — decision: depend on b744, then unify into a shared rebar.llm.criteria layer.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Remediated plan-review run 1 (BLOCK: 9 blocking / 20 advisory) + folded in the b744 code-review worktree ground-truth. Design edits: (1) concrete .rebar/criteria_routing.json overlay schema — gate-keyed, project.<name> namespace, load-time collision/rebind rejection, explicit 'activate' list (E2/G6/T8); (2) open the closed vocabularies CANONICAL_LLM + OVERLAY_IDS via effective_criteria(repo_root) — the true precondition (G6); (3) cache isolation (packaged cached, overlay uncached/mtime-keyed) + RED cross-repo-leak test (G6); (4) Attestation section — registry_version(repo_root) hashes the overlay, disable-in-coverage, expand-contract back-compat, cross-gate AC8 (G6/COH/E1); (5) DET scan scoping + LLM-block fail-open, reconciled with no-baseline (COH); (6) risk-ordered MVP build order (G5); (7) migration/compat — preserve review_code/shim.py review_result shape. Ground-truth resizes: story 3 mostly built (code_review/detectors.py via shared engine_b.scan+load_registry; generalize the hardcoded security prefix); per-criterion blocking DONE (threshold_for + kernel Pass-3); story 5 = attestation port only; review_kernel already shared by both gates. Re-running review-plan.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Plan-review CONVERGED: run 1 BLOCK (9 blocking) → run 2 (1) → run 3 (1) → run 4 (1) → run 5 PASS (0 blocking, 12 advisory, signed attestation). Each blocker was remediated into the design: overlay schema+namespace+activate+collision, open the closed vocabularies (CANONICAL_LLM/OVERLAY_IDS) via effective_criteria(repo_root) incl. the workflow Pass-1 batch vocab, cache isolation + RED cross-repo test, attestation registry_version(repo_root) threaded through all callers + disabled_builtins coverage + expand-contract, DET scan scoping + per-criterion fail_mode (open default; security closed) reconciling the fail-open/fail-closed contradiction, LLM/DET fail-open, DET prompt-less descriptor fallback shape, editor POST /criterion/preview contract + spike gate, per-criterion eval CLI + calibration metric defs, routing validation (load-time + CI), review_code/shim.py review_result preservation, risk-ordered MVP build order. Residual 12 advisories are child-level; carried into the relevant child stories. Decomposing now.

compiled_state.description:
# Project-supplied review criteria + project-invariant compliance (unified cross-gate registry)

## Context

rebar's review gates ship a fixed, built-in set of criteria. Client projects have their own
standards and invariants — naming, layering, banned APIs, architectural rules — the built-in
set can't express. This epic lets a **project supply its own review criteria** and declare
**architectural invariants** the gates check, **beyond** the built-in set.

**Cross-cutting:** it must serve **plan-review, code-review (`b744`), and any future
criteria-based gate** through **one shared registry**. (Spun out of `5fd2` as follow-on F8;
config-UX sibling `55de`/F5.)

**Posture (from `5fd2`, binding):** coach-not-block, advisory-by-default, **fail-open
(ironclad)**, polyglot / broad client base, convergent-by-construction, **reuse existing
machinery — no plugin system, no new DSL**.

## Design decisions (settled in brainstorm; see rationale comment)

1. **Unified criterion data model + shared registration/discovery/schema/eval plumbing;
   per-gate application** (gate-scoped routing inside the shared record; postures do NOT
   converge).
2. **Two authoring forms, no project code in the gate:** DET "invariants" = declarative
   pattern rules via the existing grounding scan facade; LLM criteria = prompt files.
3. **Any criterion (DET or LLM) may block** — `--force`-escapable, folds into the signed
   (kind-keyed) attestation coverage, can never rebind a built-in.
4. **Blocking is project-owned policy.** rebar surfaces the same eval/calibration instruments
   it uses on its built-ins; the maintainer decides blocking + threshold. Trust is theirs.
5. **Reuse existing formats** (prompt front-matter, the `criteria_routing.json` schema,
   Inspect-AI fixtures, the scan facade). No new formats.
6. **Explicit activation; presence != active.** Routing/threshold in a `.rebar/` overlay
   (§ Overlay schema); namespaced + additive; the overlay may re-tune or **disable** a
   built-in (recorded in coverage).
7. **Eval tooling in scope** = fixture/discrimination harness + calibration/agreement view.
   **Out** = REVIEW_RESULT FP-replay (F1; gate-agnostic, inherited later).
8. **An "invariant" is just a blocking criterion** (usually DET). One mechanism.
9. **Visual editor extended** to author project criteria (reuses `/library/create`).

## Overlay schema, activation & caching (clears E2/G6/T8)

- **File:** `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`, reusing each gate's packaged `criteria_routing.json`
  schema, keyed by gate:
  `{"plan_review": {"<id>": {…routing…}}, "code_review": {"<id>": {…}}, "activate": ["project.<name>", …]}`.
- **Per-criterion routing** reuses existing keys: plan-review `{exec, applies_at, block_threshold,
  default_posture, facet, checklist}`; code-review `{exec, applies_to (globs), block_threshold,
  blocking_enabled}`. (Note the deliberate posture divergence — plan-review derives blocking
  from `default_posture`; code-review uses an explicit `blocking_enabled`; the shared
  `threshold_for` must honor BOTH — do not "fix" the divergence.) **DET criteria also carry
  `fail_mode: open|closed`** (default `open` for project invariants; `closed` for the built-in
  security class) — see § DET-invariant scan scoping.
- **`exec` vocabulary:** `1-TURN` / `2-STEP` / `AGENT` (LLM tiers) or **`DET`** (pattern-rule
  invariant; code-review already uses `DET` inline). A **DET criterion has no prompt** — descriptor
  assembly must treat prompt resolution as OPTIONAL for `exec: DET` (today `_descriptor_from_prompt`
  unconditionally calls `get_prompt(...)` and raises if absent). A DET criterion's "rubric" is its
  detector rule id (`.rebar/detectors/`), not a `.md`; only its routing entry is required.
  **DET fallback descriptor shape** (a `_descriptor_from_prompt` DET branch that skips
  `get_prompt`): `name` = routing `name` or id; `scenario` = the detector rule's `message`/
  description from its `.rebar/detectors/<id>` envelope; `facet` = routing `facet` (default = id);
  `checklist` = empty. This is an expand-contract change (add the DET branch; existing prompt-backed
  callers unaffected).
- **Namespace / collision (load-time, located error):** a net-new project criterion id MUST be
  `project.<name>`-prefixed. An overlay entry keyed by an **un-prefixed built-in id** is a
  **re-tune/disable** of that built-in (allowed, §Design 6). A `project.`-prefixed id equal to a
  built-in id, or a duplicate project id, is **rejected at load** (a project id can never rebind
  a built-in).
- **Activation:** a criterion runs only if listed in `activate` (presence in the file ≠ active).
- **Discovery — `effective_criteria(repo_root)`** = `CANONICAL_LLM`/`OVERLAY_IDS` ∪ activated
  project ids (each resolved via `get_prompt(id, repo_root)` for its rubric + the overlay routing
  entry). Route it through **every** vocabulary site: plan-review `load_criteria`,
  `check_registry_coverage`, and the **workflow Pass-1 assemble op** (`workflow_ops` emits a
  sanitized `include_project_<name>` boolean — `.`→`_` — per activated project id as the
  coverage/routing record). **LANDED-DESIGN NOTE (ef7e):** the v3 `batch` schema is IMMUTABLE and
  the gate self-validates, so project criteria do NOT fan out via a new workflow field; instead the
  rebar-specific `ProductionBatchRunner` appends the `project.` subset of `route_criteria` to the
  finder set (built-ins keep their static YAML `criteria:` slots, unchanged). Code-review sites
  (`OVERLAY_IDS`, `routing_index`, `threshold_for`) are unified later, after b744 (story 5065).
- **Caching (clears G6 cache bug) — LANDED (ef7e):** `_routing_index()` stays `@lru_cache`d for the
  **packaged** index (immutable per binary); `effective_routing(repo_root)` / `load_criteria` are
  `@lru_cache(maxsize=128)` keyed by `(repo_root, sha256(overlay-bytes))` — a CONTENT signature
  (not mtime), so an overlay edit self-invalidates and cross-repo leakage is impossible;
  `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()` clears them. A RED cross-repo-leak test proves it.

## Attestation & invalidation (clears G6/COH/E1; reuses kind-keyed attest infra)

- **`registry_version(repo_root=None)`** additionally hashes the `.rebar/` overlay when
  `repo_root` is given; thread `repo_root` through **every** caller — `claim_gate_check →
  compute_validity`, and also `drift_refresh_candidate` and `remediation_mode_candidate` (which
  call `registry_version()` today without it) — so activating/editing/disabling a criterion
  **invalidates a prior attestation at claim time** (today it hashes packaged routing only — the
  change does not invalidate).
- **Disable recorded in coverage:** a disabled built-in is listed in a `disabled_builtins: [ids]`
  field in the verdict `coverage` dict (and folded into the signed manifest) so the cert reflects
  reduced coverage rather than silent omission; the field is additive/expand-contract (absent on
  old certs).
- **Cross-gate:** applies to **any gate attestation backed by the shared registry** (plan-review
  now; code-review once it signs — see Migration; code-review signs nothing today).
- **Expand-contract / back-compat:** the new regver basis is additive; an existing attestation
  lacking it is treated as **stale → re-review**, never a crash. Overlay absent ⇒ regver == today
  (no invalidation churn).

## DET-invariant scan scoping & fail-open (clears COH)

- A DET invariant runs as a **whole-repo content scan** via the shared `grounding.engine_b.scan`.
  The **oracle always fails open** (engine binary absent / parse-error / timeout → `abstain`). The
  **consumer** then applies the criterion's **`fail_mode`**: `open` (default for project invariants)
  turns an abstain into a skip = PASS; `closed` (the built-in security class, via the existing
  `apply_failclosed`) turns an abstain into a BLOCK. So generalizing `code_review/detectors.py` =
  making its currently-hardcoded fail-closed behavior a per-criterion `fail_mode` that **defaults
  open** — the two behaviors are one code path parameterized by `fail_mode`, not a contradiction.
  Matches are **gate-scoped**: code-review filters matches to the **diff** (as
  `code_review/detectors.py` already does); plan-review has no diff, so a DET invariant checks the
  plan's declared intent and repo context — an invariant that inherently needs a diff is **N/A at
  plan time (fail-open)** and belongs to code-review.
- Because matches are gate-scoped (diff / plan), pre-existing whole-repo violations are **not**
  surfaced as new work — this is why **no baseline/grandfathering** is needed (reconciles the
  Out-of-scope note).
- **LLM fail-open:** an LLM criterion configured to block whose call errors/times out **fails
  open** (skipped, does not block) — same rule as DET.

- **`apply_failclosed` → `fail_mode` expand-contract (clears T4; story `7f0d`).** The generalization
  is backward-compatible, no flag-day: `apply_failclosed(...)` is KEPT as a thin wrapper that calls
  the new `apply_fail_mode(..., fail_mode="closed")` — so its **default stays `closed`** and every
  existing caller (`code_review/detectors.py`, the built-in security class) is byte-identical until
  explicitly migrated. `detectors.py` is then migrated to call the new function with the
  criterion's routing `fail_mode` (project invariants default `open`, security class `closed`); the
  old wrapper is deprecated and removed only after all callers pass an explicit `fail_mode`.
  **Rollback** = revert callers to the `apply_failclosed` wrapper (still `closed`). No caller's
  behavior changes silently.

## What exists vs. what we build (grounded to file:line, 2026-06-30; incl. b744 worktree)

**Already built (reuse):**
- **Shared three-pass kernel** `review_kernel/` (verify/decide/coach) — **both** gates already
  consume it (code-review via `code_review/workflow_ops.py`); the divergence-risky core is
  already unified.
- **A DET pattern-rule → finding consumer already exists:** `code_review/detectors.py` calls the
  shared `grounding.engine_b.scan` + `load_registry` (which already unions `.rebar/detectors/`) +
  `apply_failclosed` (consumer-side fail-CLOSED; oracle stays fail-OPEN). Generalizable — the only
  hardcoded bits are the `rebar.builtin.security.` prefix + 2 criterion mappings.
- **Per-criterion blocking is DONE:** `code_review/registry.threshold_for` + kernel Pass-3 +
  detector fail-closed.
- **Project extension points:** `.rebar/prompts/` (rubric override), `.rebar/<gate>_moves.json`
  (additive coach moves), `.rebar/detectors/` (project pattern rules), `.rebar/evals/` (fixtures).
- **Kind-keyed attestation infra** (`plan_review/attest.py`); Inspect-AI eval seam; discrimination
  scorers + Cohen's κ + parity metrics.

**Net-new (build):**
- **Open the closed criterion vocabularies** (`CANONICAL_LLM`, `OVERLAY_IDS`) via
  `effective_criteria(repo_root)` — THE precondition; a project criterion is silently skipped
  today regardless of overlay.
- **`.rebar/` routing overlay + activation + cache isolation** (§ Overlay schema).
- **A shared descriptor abstraction:** code-review has only `routing_index()` (raw routing), no
  `load_criteria`-style merged descriptor and no `facet/checklist/…`; unification introduces/
  generalizes one and reconciles the `blocking_enabled` vs `default_posture` postures + exec-tier-
  polymorphic (prompt-less DET vs LLM) criteria.
- **Generalize the DET-scan consumer** beyond the security prefix (data-driven criterion↔detector
  map) so arbitrary project invariants route; make the fail-open/closed behavior a per-criterion
  `fail_mode` (today `apply_failclosed` is hardcoded closed; default project invariants to open);
  expose to plan-review too.
- **Per-criterion eval runner** — `eval_solver.run_case` is a closed 3-id dispatch (raises
  otherwise) + a validate-only CLI; add a criterion-aware runner + a live CLI + the
  **calibration/agreement view** (rendering is net-new; κ/parity metrics exist).
- **Attestation port to code-review** + `registry_version(repo_root)` + disable-coverage +
  claim-gate invalidation (§ Attestation).
- **Editor live must-fire/must-not-fire preview** — a new **synchronous** editor endpoint
  `POST /criterion/preview` `{criterion_id | inline(prompt+routing), fixture}` →
  `{verdict: "fire"|"no-fire", finding?: <findings.py finding shape>, rationale: str}`, reusing
  the per-criterion eval runner behind the `agents` extra + credentials, with a **configurable
  timeout (default 60s)** (no prompt-execution endpoint exists today — the editor's `POST` set is
  `/save`, `/prompt/save`, `/validate`, `/library/create`). **Spike gate:** if a single sync
  preview does not return within the timeout on the reference model, fall back to an async
  job+poll shape rather than blocking the editor's `ThreadingHTTPServer` handler.
- **Routing derivation/validation pipeline** (`criteria_routing.json` is hand-maintained; no
  generator/drift gate).

## Migration & compatibility

- **Callsites** to route through `effective_criteria`: plan-review `load_criteria`,
  `check_registry_coverage`, `attest.registry_version`, `workflow_ops` vocab; code-review
  `OVERLAY_IDS`, `routing_index`, `threshold_for`.
- **Preserve the `review_code` / `code_review/shim.py` `review_result` return shape** — it is a
  back-compat surface consumed by MCP `review_code`, CLI `rebar review-code`, and the Gerrit
  `review_bot/adapter.py`.
- **Rollback = fail-open to packaged:** overlay absent ⇒ both gates behave exactly as today. The
  unification is expand-contract; a misbehaving unified registry falls back per-gate. The
  `registry_version` code change is itself expand-contract (old certs → stale, never crash).
- **Docs deliverable:** an ADR under `docs/adr/` (LANDED: `docs/adr/0015-project-supplied-criteria.md` for the plan-review slice) + a `CLAUDE.md` "project-supplied criteria"
  section document the unified registry contract, the overlay merge/activation semantics, and the
  attestation-invalidation protocol, plus a how-to for authoring/activating/eval'ing/blocking on a
  criterion (the concrete target for the docs AC).

## Build order (risk-ordered MVP; clears G5 thin-slice)

Prove the full loop on the riskiest piece first, then fan out:
1. **MVP slice — ✅ DONE (story `ef7e`, PR #103).** `effective_criteria`/`effective_routing`
   (content-hash cache isolation) → activate ONE project LLM criterion from the `.rebar/` overlay →
   `ProductionBatchRunner` fans it into Pass-1 → surfaces its finding; CI parity gate + ADR 0015.
   (The `registry_version`-reflects-the-overlay + attestation-invalidation half is split into story
   `08af`, step 6 below — this slice proved author→activate→run→surface.)
2. Generalize the DET-invariant scan consumer (data-driven map).
3. Per-criterion eval runner + calibration view.
4. Editor live-preview + authoring extension.
5. **Unify** plan-review + code-review registries into `rebar.llm.criteria` (after `b744` lands).

## Dependency

**Blocked on `b744`** (code-review; WS1–WS5 landed on `worktree-code-review-capability`, WS6 =
Gerrit voter pending). The `review_kernel` is already shared; the remaining fork is the
registry/descriptor + posture + vocabulary layer, unified after b744 lands (temporary
duplication accepted).

## Success Criteria

- A project can add its own criteria (DET pattern-rules + LLM prompts) that run in plan-review,
  code-review, and future gates via **one shared registry**.
- A project can declare architectural invariants (blocking DET criteria) the gates check.
- The project **owns blocking/threshold policy** per criterion, informed by the same
  eval/calibration instruments rebar uses on its built-ins.
- **No project code executes in the gate**; unsupported stacks fail open.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `effective_criteria(repo_root)` opens the closed vocabularies (`CANONICAL_LLM` +
      `OVERLAY_IDS`) so an activated project criterion is loaded/run by the gate; routed through
      every vocabulary callsite (see Migration).
- [ ] `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay per the § Overlay schema: gate-keyed, namespaced,
      explicit `activate` list, load-time collision/rebind rejection (a located error).
- [ ] Overlay merge is cache-isolated per repo (packaged index stays cached; overlay is not); a
      RED test proves no cross-repo routing leakage.
- [ ] The overlay may re-tune or disable a built-in; a disable is recorded in attestation coverage.
- [ ] Project DET pattern-rule invariants run through the generalized (data-driven)
      `engine_b.scan` consumer and surface as blocking-capable findings; fail-open on
      engine-binary-absent / parse-error / timeout per the criterion's `fail_mode` (default `open`
      → skip = PASS; security-class `closed` → block).
- [ ] Project LLM prompt criteria run through the shared registry with gate-scoped routing; an
      LLM-block criterion that errors fails open. … [truncated by reconciler]
compiled_state.title: Project-supplied review criteria + project-invariant compliance (unified cross-gate registry)
